channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 94 replies — 376.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-05-07 16:24 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188593
>>62179622
>You fags are cooked (including me)

62188936
>>62184343
Surplus: Norway, Denmark
Minor deficit: Sweden

62192220
>>62188593
Yummy

62193530
>>62179629
Food
>America is food secure
Unti AI agriculture was the most subsidized industry in the country.

62193681
>>62184336
Just re-arrange american healthcare and social security system. Private healthcare and private education are incredibly costly.

62193707
>>62184336
The system more than likely is incapable of being fixed at this point and cutting benefits is political suicide.

62193761
>>62193707
The fix is inflation. They'll keep running it hot to inflate away the debt while forcing bond rates lower artificially.

62194225
>>62179622
Doesn't matter its all fabricated when the fed can print infinitely

62194394
>>62179629
The world stopping to use of the US dollar as the de-facto reserve currency and universal trade tender is not something the US in its current state can survive tho.

Any other country in the world would be facing the greatest hyperinflation event ever seen if they did the shit the US is doing. They're the only things barely preventing it right now.

62194405
>>62184336
you assume things aren't being played out exactly as intended

62197937
>>62179622
me when bi dl trade verbal dom

62200684
>>62179622
>The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy

is that bad? Most people have more debt than cash in the bank, not a big deal! Plus, Trump loves debt. Its all part of the plan.

62200692
>>62179928
i'm with this guy. eat shit commies

62200774
>>62200684
The sovereign bond ponzi scheme rolling over depends on the active population out numbering the inactive population that demands monthly checks for doing nothing. The inactive population is outnumbering the active population 20:1. No that's not sustainable anymore. The risk the debt carries is irrational the debt is uncovered, it would need to be defaulted on , respective bought up by a bad bank. The former is the dead of the global ponziconomy the latter is asking for the active population defecting and making the debt even more uncovered

The sovereign bond ponzis are over

62200945
>>62179630
Yeah I don't think anyone voted for bank bailouts, PPP loans, corporate tax breaks or the 1.5 trillion dollar military budget

62200948
>>62200684
>people are the same as national economies
RETARD ALARM

62200952
>>62179928
You dumb fuck the debt isn't something that you take from no country whatsoever the point is nobody is lending you any money and then you uscuck are finished

62200965
>>62180253
nigger if we stop paying our debt the entire world's economy collapses and china becomes the new superpower overnight. going to bomb some shithole is not changing any of that

62200972
>>62180253
yeah sure get usabugs nuked for good retarded fuck

62200980
>>62200945
it's more like the government puts up a facade of employment while taking out 3 trillion every year. just dismantle this unconstitutional ass government

62201141
>>62179622
It’s just time for this country to go and fade away and fall asleep forever, hopefully the death throes of a dying empire will be quick with little collateral dmg

62201178
The amount of debt genuinely doesn’t matter at all because the currency keeps inflating, and the currency keeps inflating because we keep injecting money into the system via govt spending, which gets us further in debt. It’s a cyclical infinite money glitch and nobody will stop it because it’s just the way a system that uses fiat currency works

62201273
>>62201178
If the debt doesn't matter Marx, then why doesn't the gubermint just default on the uncovered ponzi schemes and instead needs to force legislate private actors to buy up the toxic trash bond waste

62201365
>>62201273
because that would destroy the legitimacy of the system and they couldn't keep doing it. why the fuck would they do that?

62201624
>>62179622
They will inflate away the debt while keeping the dollar relevant as long as possible by massive military spending (which accomplishes both goals.) The draft will return while American citizens become increasingly desperate and impoverished and the debt gets inflated into nothing. As foreign demand for dollars collapses they will force 401ks and pension funds to buy them for the greater good, and everyone who doesn't own assets will be wiped out. S&P will hit ATH after ATH while bread becomes $50 a loaf. Finally after WW3 they'll restructure the whole economic system into something else altogether.

62202110
>>62201178
yeah and if the currency inflates it's worth nothing, the us economy crumbles and you fat fucks are going to be paying 20 times more for your burgies

62202185
>>62179629
>americans 2 hours after chinks stop selling to them

62202241
If there was one good thing COVID could have done, then it would have been killing all the old people (ages 65 and up) and the people with comorbidities. Honestly, the decrease in entitlement payments would have really helped the budget for all countries. In the long run, even with the unnecessary lockdowns, the event would have been net positive. Instead we got a faggy lock down and transferred more wealth to the old fags.

62202250
>>62202241
every country got a huge debt because of boomers once again when it was the perfect timing to get rid of them

62202273
>>62202241
That's true but unfortunately most of the developed world is a gerontocracy anon, which is why society got shut down and the youth missed all their developmental milestones for 2 years so the old could feel safer from the flu.

item.title: The LINK token has value — 109 replies — 160.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-05-07 15:43 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62192984
>>62192888
>duuuuuuuude everyone else needs to be truthful except us


hey kid, did you notice your cult stopped using the ollll truth>trust after they got called out?

they grifted you, and here you are begging for their approval

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62192988
>>62192925
>duuuuuuuuude one of the top grifters in crypto history charles said my cult was expensive

there you have it folks, irrefutable truth

62193165
>>62192940
>>62192984
>>62192988
I mean you don't have to believe it or look it up even.

62193169
>>62176477
do you have any response to >>62176598 ?
If you don't that proves you are either
A) stupid bot
B) stupid nigerian
C) angry fudder
D) link holder trying desperately to fud others away from the best moonshot there is.
in any case, everyone can just ignore you: )

62193244
>>62192982
nigga no one is breeding with you KEK

>>62192988
or you lul

brownies rekt once again

62193356
>>62176295
Yes anon. One last step. It's always one more thing. Linkies always dangling new carrots to scam new anons. Get fucked, baggie

62195762
>>62193356
hi! stay mad about link, post in link threads too
>>62192854
>where is the proof from all those off-chain payments
"guy just give me the list of private business transactions. you know the ones where chainlink has negotiated price and terms with each private business partner"
You can see that the Chainlink reserve continues to grow, and payment abstraction means link is bought with onchain and offchain payments. To know more, you'll need to search.

62195773
>>62195762
Chainlink understands how privacy is needed for industry, too

62196215
Pretty sad how the LINK shills cling onto their delusion of financial freedom in the year 2017+9

62196318
>>62177980
Its kinda crazy that the whole amount of link left is only worth 2b while ripple dumps this every month lmao

62196466
>>62196318
They should sell it all at once so we can buy the crash and make it during the ensuing bullrun

62199072
>>62192644
Yeah no one has confirmed it
... except the actual parties themselves saying directly in interviews that they're paying for it

kill yourself, subhuman faggot

62199117
>>62195762
I get what you're doing anon, but the responses just seem pointless. I used to get into these "debates" with these types, but you realize these people either hold massive stacks themselves and have a number of reasons they do this, or they're mentally ill, and there's no point because they'll never stop.

Just look at it from an incentive perspective, lets say they don't hold link and they're just trying to have a debate, OK, why waste more time than needed to tell someone they're wrong? They don't do this in xrp threads, or literally anything else on this board. So at that point they're mentally ill and you're wasting your time.

OK, let's say they do hold link. Well, they're either fudding in the 4chan style of "I hate people liking what I like" and trying to gatekeep, in which case they'll never break because this is what people do here. Or they are angry because they bought high and trying to rationalize with themselves why they haven't sold, in which case nothing you say will ease their pain because only the price going up can make them happy.

Nothing about them is good faith, it screams either trolling, mental illness, or bitter resentment, possibly a combination of all 3 in some cases. And at this point, the people who understand link and know what's being built aren't enthused enough to counter this, so unless you want to commit the same sort of negative mental energy, they'll just overwhelm you with quantity over quality.

62199240
>>62199117
>don't argue with holders
>don't argue with non-holders
>just stay in our ebin echo chamber
>let's just XRPmaxx
Interesting

62199263
>fuuuuck every siiiingle stiiiiink

62199326
>>62199117
having fudders in a thread is usually bullish, and I enjoy quickly replying to them!

62199336
>linkie pisses himself
>dude thats gross
>linkie: i won

62199852
>>62184641
their business model is genius. doesnt benefit holders of the funding token at all, but you cant say it its not a brilliant model for CLL profits

62199891
>>62199852
>doesnt benefit holders of the funding token at all
it does, the funding token, LINK, is staked and used as payment for oracle nodes. someone could try to pay nodes to break the network. Without link, the network doesn't have full economic security.
>>62176214
there are more reasons LINK has value that are not listed here

62199896
>>62199891

62199899
>>62199336
You scared of a little bit of pee? The pharma industry has raped your mind to prevent you from drinking the elixer of the gods.

62200039
>>62199240
Nobody's in an echo chamber fuckhead, its a hurtbox, there hasn't been a positive no fud link thread on this board in at least 5 years. I'm just saying most of the people fudding aren't doing it in good faith, they're just bitching for a variety of reasons related to impatience, trolling, and mental illness. Or they're too cowardly to follow their own ambition, sell, and move on with their life. Nobody's shilling you link anon, and based on your posts in this thread you sound deeply unhappy, my advice to you would be to sell and focus on something else, its clear you either need money right away or simply do not believe there's a long term play here.

62200053
>>62200039
we fuddies are the rich chads who are living life to the fullest. i just got back from a WORLD CRUISE dude. what have you been up to?

62200616
>>62200053

62200814
>>62199326
Cool, but can you show the CCIP revenue chart, pretty please?

62200835
>>62192801
what are you talking about??

>https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/polymarket-partners-with-chainlink-to-enhance-accuracy-of-prediction-market-resolutions-302555123.html

they even said they're looking into replacing UMA for non-price based prediction too.

the biggest prediction market on solana adopted chainlink too

>https://x.com/chainlink/status/2031354239474176035

theres another one too i cant remember the name off, but you could have googled this in literally 2 seconds instead of coping

62200993
>>62200814
https://www.cciptracker.com/
You can watch in real time

62201004
>>62199326
nice chart cuckold. Check the price :)

62201058
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!!!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62202072
>>62199117
>guy with no gains in half a decade writes a psych 101 paper rationalizing why people are discussing something on a discussion board

>duuuuuuuuuuude just donate and don't ask questions

>worships a "god" with a fake beard who pretends to know about banking


THE Cuckolds of crypto

item.title: I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in.

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-07 16:13 UTC

OP

I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely. I might as well not exist at all. It’s like i’m the biggest loser in high school or something. The people here who advocate for the NEET lifestyle must be some real freaks.

Early replies (first 3)

62201436
I am truly sorry to hear that you dont have anything meaningful to do in your life or have an identity besides being a wageslave. This is entirely a (you) problem

62201447
Apartment? Let me guess youre living in some shitty city surrounded by brown people in some shitty apartment and youre wondering why your life is shit. You need to fix your environment, you do not need to hypnotize yourself by working some bullshit job that you dont care about

62201460
>>62201428
I'm sorry to say that you've been brainwashed. If you can't do anything but work then you should kill yourself. Actually don't because we need people like you to do "important things" like putting our fries in the bag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62201557
>>62201543
Yeah society is totally worth beeing takes care of... Totally important...

62201578
>>62201428
The only time I'm moving away from my house is to live in a mega city.
You just live poor

62201588
>>62201464
>workout
>cook healthy food
>clean up place
>go for walks/hike/bike
>go kayaking/canoeing/jet skiing

Yeah these are nice but ultimately i still feel fucking lonely, like i’m just selfimproooving without any reason for it. While i could be learning valuable skills at work.

>work on some project
>research cryptos
>tweet multiple times per day shilling your coins

This is just work but with a shitty uncertain pay-off.

>unironically have sex

Women have a lot more sex with you when you work, especially if it’s a high status, high paying job.

>shit post on the Chans
>play vidya
>watch podcasts and shit

Are you unironically 16? How does an adult find this in any way a good way to spend time? Except as a price for hard work.

>go to church
>read sacred scripture
>read about the saints
Yeah religion has some nice philosophy in it but most of it is a waste of time that could be spend in work actually helping people.

All in all i don’t understand how you can just enjoy wasting time without any purpose. It’s nice to take a few months long vacation every now and then if you’re feeling really exhausted, but other than that just no.

62201612
>>62201555
>>62201557
You guys just sound like you were retarded enough to get mindbroken by the internet information flood. Our ancestors would have fought many wars in order to be able to have the living standards we have in our societies.

>>62201578
>>62201447
City or countryside, which is it? I’m from the countryside, it really isn’t much different doing nothing at all in a forest. Walks are nice but you need a purpose besides them too. I have money saved so i can live upper middle class lifestyle for three months before my next work starts. All in all this just feels fucking lonely, like how a very unpopular loser kid would live.

62201672
>>62201612
>We shall fight to the last man to ensure that our children are replaced from their own homeland and their work devalued to the point they cannot even afford shelter, but at least they have glowing rectangles to stare at while everything collapses

62201684
>>62201612
Living standards? Who cares about that shit as long as there is food which is guaranteed by modern technological farming? They would be shocked seeing we get invaded by third worlders while having feminism.

62201774
>>62201612
>Our ancestors would have fought many wars in order to be able to have the living standards we have in our societies.
Plumbing, medicine, and maybe electricity. Everything else has been a straight shoot down. The food we eat shit we drink is pure poison, local communities are gone and replaced with nigger/kurdish gangs, and we spend nearly all our salary on rent. Our ancestors are more likely to start a war to end this sad state of affairs, not to win it.

62201790
>>62201612
If my ancestors had to work a 9 to 5 factory job for 5 to 6 days a week for minimum wage they would kill themselves or start a revolution.

62201817
Because you are a boring faggot who has only been conditioned to work and can't fathom doing anything else.
I haven't worked since 2020 and my life is full of fun and joy.

62201851
>>62201428
Pathetic state of mind. Choose something that sucks in your life and do something every day to improve it.
Take 6 months go travel/hiking and eat some psychedelics if you're this lost without being told what to do every day.
You will find the most satisfaction from putting your effort towards benefiting others.

62201888
>>62201428
>alone in apartment
>don’t have to do shit
>bitching about it

Do amerigolem really?
Most of reality is not other people

62201889
Feeling you, OP.
Fortunately social welfare is mind-blowingly good in my country. It's actually fucking insane. Can't complain about the NEET lifestyle.
That said, I'd love to work, but there is apparently fucking none. My country will probably implode within short notice, so there is that.

62201910
>>62201428
I am a farmer now and get to choose my own hours for the most part but I used to work in factories full time before my dad retired from farming. I hated working full time and will perform samurai ritual sudoku on myself in minecraft if I ever went broke and couldn't farm for a living. I don't think work would be so bad if the days were not so long. I think 4 shifts of 6 hours would be nice because 6 hours was always the point in the day I started to have dark thoughts.

Now that I decide how much work I'm going to do I usually just work 3-4 hours per day. Obviously during planting and harvest I will work more and I work nearly every waking moment until this is done. This work is made tolerable by knowing that once it is done it is done for the year so I have a very specific goal instead of constantly working hard and just having more stuff piled on.

62201945
>>62201543
>”my spreadsheet job is VERY IMPORTANT OKAY?!”

Just fucking lol

62201955
>>62201889
Guten Tag.

62201961
>>62201945
Thats what Career Women unironically think.

62201966
>>62201955
Bingo uezs.
Kann mich persönlich nicht beschweren, aber dieses Land ist unironisch am Arsch.

62201969
>>62201851
>Choose something that sucks in your life and do something every day to improve it.

Nothing sucks that much in my life and as i already said, self inprovement without purpose feels meaningless

>You will find the most satisfaction from putting your effort towards benefiting others

Yeah exactly, that’s why working is so great.

>>62201790
Is everyone here some kind of retarded? You think people were forced to that kind of work? They immigrated from the farms to cities willingly. I actually have friends who grow up in farms and they gladly choose 9-5 over the 24/7/365 at the farm.

>>62201774
>>62201684
See above. Also our ancerstors didn’t care much about nationalism or race, empire’s used to be more multicultural, spanning many different cultures and ethnicities. Immigration has always existed, it’s nothing new either.

>>62201684
>feminism

Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore! Only low value men care about that. Do only low value men like neeting?

62201972
>>62201588
>Are you unironically 16? How does an adult find this in any way a good way to spend time? Except as a price for hard work.
are you unironically a slave?
>All in all i don’t understand how you can just enjoy wasting time without any purpose
you have been brainwashed
working a job is just picking a purpose that serves someone else. take the money out of the equation and surely you can see how cooking a healthy meal and sitting in quiet contemplation is more purposeful for your own devices than completing tasks that don't matter to you
you complain about lack of connection, but i'm willing to bet you haven't tried volunteering somewhere nearby because it's unpaid work, right?
you might try to dress your anxiety up as "wishing to serve a purpose" but its really just a constantly gnawing itch to get more money. that's understandable given the environment that we're in, but i think it helps everyone to be honest with what we're dealing with when stating complaints
>>62201945
i work office jobs and the amount of people so far up their ass about things that are completely pointless (even within the context of getting their pointless jobs done) is incredibly delusional. it used to bother me a little, but now i'm just impressed that level of disconnect from reality can be sustained and even rewarded for so long

62201984
>>62201969
>Also our ancerstors didn't care much about nationalism or race, empire's used to be more multicultural, spanning many different cultures and ethnicities. Immigration has always existed, it's nothing new either.
Even reddit fags don't believe this anymore.

62202019
>>62201945
Why do you want to assume my job? I’m a doctor.

>>62201889
I’m also from europe and yeah it’s sad that because we don’t force people to struggle and thus invent not needed jobs just to survive, there are less jobs to go around. Also lol at you saying Germany will collapse, it will finally have it’s world dominating empire in the form of EU now that USA and Russia have totally shat the bed.

>>62201910
I guess you don’t have animals? But you’re special, as i said almost all my friends chose the 9-5. But at least you work. People writing here dream of not working at all.

62202041
>>62201984
Care to refute this? Roman empire, Persian empire, 1100 ad Britain, all multicultural and full of different ethnicities.

62202055
>>62202041
You're retarded and wrong.

62202071
>>62202041

62202075
>>62201969
>feels meaningless
Probably so apparent from your first phrase. Stuck in head, ego, I bet you think *you* have *your thoughts* right? in *your* mind?
The illusion becomes easier to escape with practice.

62202092
>>62202019
>it will finally have it’s world dominating empire in the form of EU
Do you actually believe the nonsense Brussels tells the world 24/7 in utter disregard for reality?
That's almost as bad as trusting the Kremlin even a single word.
If I had to bet on who will lead the world a few years down the line, it will be countries that haven't attempted to kill their economy (and by extension their people) for absolutely insane ideological reasons, while suppressing any and all opposition.
So probably the US, Argentine, Japan and whoever else isn't fucked in the head. The US obviously only in case the Democratic Party doesn't burn it to the ground (in "mostly peaceful protest"!), because Americans keep voting for "Anti-Democrats".

62202094
>>62202055
>>62202071
Damn chuds are retarded

62202108
>>62202092
I see, everyone advocating for neetdom really has a two digit iq. Brussels isn’t in control of what people post on twitter and tiktok lmao. They aren’t some kind of autocracy.

62202135
>>62202108
You don't even follow who you are replying to, despite /biz/ having IDs.
I don't know what the other guys have posted and I am also not interested in it, but the retard is probably (You).

62202208
>>62201436
fpbp and
/thread

item.title: ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT… — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-07 16:22 UTC

OP

ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT
STOP SELLING
HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER
40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT BEFORE SPREADING FUCK
I LITERALLY JUST BOUGHT BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62202000
Fuck off retard

62202033
>>62201978
>“it is possible more cases will be reported” because it can be up to six weeks before those infected with the Andes variant of hantavirus show symptoms.

Lmao 40% mortality with SIX WEEKS to transmit it would fucking DESTROY humanity if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.

62202089
>>62202033
Bullish on Hanta? Truly a nothing burger though, this disease is likely less transmissible than herpes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62202140
>>62202033
>hopium

62202144
>>62202089
I think it's probably a nothingburger, but at the same time you're dealing with a strain that's evolving and we don't fully understand at this point. If we see any propogation of the virus beyond the cruise ship I'd be very concerned.

I made $3MM trading otm options on COVID off of $20k , biologist by training.

62202190
>>62202033
But it's not infectious without symptoms. The few reports that were done on this showed that it's infectous from human to human for literally a single day, which is the day with high fever. So yeah the chance of this becoming a hard to contain pandemic is basically zero like this.

62202196
>>62201978
why are they like this

62202221
>Price is rejected by the 200 day moving average
>quick run the current thing fud!!!!
The goyim are cattle

62202258
>>62202190
That's what they first said about COVID.

Fact is all of this is rapidly developing based on just a handful of cases and what people claim they know about the virus can change quite quickly.

What you want to look for is deaths and confirmed cases, especially outside the cruise ship. Keeping in mind the six week delay here, if we see growing *confirmed* cases at an exponential rate, especially those outside the ship, brace your fucking self because there will be a shitstorm in another month+.

Odds are again it's a nothingburger, but if we see multiple degrees of human-to-human transmission that's when you buckle down

item.title: You kids are gonna experience dotcom — 17 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
You kids are gonna experience dotcom

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-07 15:31 UTC

OP

You can thank the grifter in chief for dumping so much liquidity and bullshit tweets for the algos that we are actually going to get THE blowoff top as CPI is set to print 4.5% next week

Couldn’t be a more fitting ending. I mean, how else could you top bankrupting a casino?

Crassus would blush

Early replies (first 3)

62200123
Dotcom and gfc are going to look like a mild correction compared to 60 years of pyramid ponzi scheming on a deflating demographic popping. The bond market is 2 months away from literally dying

62200131
>>62200106
never happening the govt will keep pumping the markets it will never crash like dotcom ever again.

62200139
>>62200131
Aha, that's why the legislator is already so desperate that it forces private companies to buy into the bankrupt sovereign bond ponzi scheme. Lol

62200236

62200242

62200621

item.title: It’s not a bubble — 22 replies — 17.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
It’s not a bubble

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-07 15:08 UTC

OP

It’s just a new paradigm

>As of April 2026, Anthropic's annualized revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion, driven by enterprise API usage, up from approximately $9 billion in late 2025. While rapidly growing, the company is not currently profitable due to massive capital expenditures on AI infrastructure, including a $200 billion, 5-year commitment to cloud services.

Early replies (first 3)

62198820
>ratio
Advertising account that gets swamped into feeds and ignored and likely blocked the moment any human comes across. Irrelevant. Go advertise your ad account somewhere else

62198822
>promise to buy mag7 slop
>mag7 earnings go up
>mag7 then commit to buy more of your slop
>everybody wins

“Not circular”

62198831
>>62198820

Take your medicine

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62198832
and OPis also confirmed a bot.
4chan is worse than moltbook

62198861
>>62198820
>>62198832
Meds

62198865
Who are you social signaling to bot, yourself?

62198890
>>62198817
This is the AI supercycle. Nasdaq is going to 40k. 10x long now or get left behind.

62198903
>>62198890
>my index scam that mostly went number up due to a demographic group that turned negative cashflow last year is going to keep going up
>please buy my bag
how about no. Pension funds have no other option left anymore to fulfil their contractual obligations than to sell into a non existent bid

62200785
>>62198890
Let's say that AI within the next 5-10 years will bring a massive boost to GDP, not just in a numerical sense. But the AI actually produce or help produce things.
This growth of the economy will not be evenly distributed, at the top of my mind then you will have three categories of companies 1) picks and shovels, think Nvidia, Anthropic etc. Companies whose product is AI to some extend. 2) Those whose product can be made by AI, a double edged sword, but a web page design company could either die or benefit significantly from AI. 3) those that will see little, except from "rising tide lift all boats", McDonalds or Walmart will probably see little benefit from AI.

My point is that Nasdaq is a mixed bags of 1,2 and 3. Think the money is in 1, and the big money is in finding a 2 that survive e.g. video game developer that use live-LLM NPC's.

62200795
>>62200785
Thinking mistake. If the demand facing the increased offer is declining the effect on the voodoo numbers of GDP are muted to negative. Ai is a deflation accelerating technology

62200855
>>62200795
Maybe with time.. I could see a scenario where AI leads to increased competitiveness, that the profit margin shrinks. That the barrier to create a game like GTA-6 will get so low, that a hundred of similar quality games will be released every year, and the video game designers will begin competing on price, because the supply is greater than the demand.

But even if this is the case, i still think that AI will reshuffle our economy to such an extend that real money is to be made. A lot of horse-carriage and analogue cameras manufactures has to die. Seems impossible for the AI-enabled-successors of such companies not to capture a real customer base with real money to spend. Especially in the early stages, where the competitive landscape is still immature.

62200858
I'm applying to every dumbass AI job I can because they're remote and no one else is hiring besides the warehouse, so I hope it's not a bubble.

62200878
>>62200858
ai jobs are actually good to applied to because they ask for 5-10 years of experience but no one has it in this particular sector

just get 2-3 certifications and you are good to go

62201449
>>62198822
It is circular but it has to be because nobody else has that kind of money to throw at a start-up

62201474
>>62201449
so you are aware the stock market and pseudo valuations of the ponzi shares is just a circular re-hypotecization ponzi scheme that is 100% dependent on the collateral not going tits up. Then you understand that ponzi is at the end of its life with the primary underlying collateral, the sovereign ponzi scheme deterministically dying due to demographic realities and the majority cashflow and credit demand that came from an overhang of geriatric demographic that was a net positive cashflow for the past 40 years now having turned into a net negative cashflow

Anybody still holding onto Boomer ponzis in the late 2020s is asking to be fucked in the ass and lose literally everything

62201503
>>62200855
Is "our" economy in the room with you now?
you fucking idiots simply dont get offer and demand like the typical communist scum. The "economy" of the past 60 years on a GLOBAL SCALE was never an economy but a purely on hopes, dreams, overrolled unvovered sovereign bond ponzis and the assumption of an ever growing base of suckers based ponziconomy.

You cant solve demand side issues on the production side no matter how much more efficient you make productivity. If there is a diminishing demand, be it for goods and services or credit demand the ponzi is cooked. The fantasy valuations go line down, the potential technological productivity increase is simply not needed, let alone is able to make it so the valuation of the collateral doesn't go line down as well, collapsing the entire ponzi and burring the boomer paradigms and plans under it

62201529
the worst of all this shit is that I did not buy motherfucker MUU when it dropped to 263 before rocketing to 470 after earnings. That's ONE FUCKING WEEK and it nearly 60%.

62201637
>>62198817
a 100% legit look at op for the last 2 years

62201638
>>62201474
>so you are aware the stock market and pseudo valuations of the ponzi shares is just a circular re-hypotecization ponzi scheme that is 100% dependent on the collateral not going tits up.
Yes you fucking retard, stock prices depend on the companies not going bankrupt.

62201644
>>62201503
you should get more mad and blog more on 4chan for free.

62201687
>>62201638
wrong, stock valuation depends on a sucker buying out somebody trying to cash out. In the concrete case today that means a pension fund that has a legal obligation to pay out liquidity to a geriatric demographic overhang has somehow to find buyers for that ponzi paper boomer Joe bought for his 401K 40 years ago in a market with less buyers. Offer and demand, if the demand is lower than the offer the price goes down; if that price is already a function of a re-hypotecization pyramid ponzi scheme, independent of the company being bankrupt or not, producing something or not, the stock valuation has a real probability going to literally 0

62201886
>>62198817
Anyone that's used Claude code knows it's a useful and powerful product.

Whether the financial instruments setup to fund these overpriced nvidia gpus and strained power grids actually sustains itself is a seperate question- but unless you're owning stock it doesn't really matter.

Anthropic could go bust tomorrow. That's not going to mean their sonnet and opus models are going to get deleted. That's not going to mean the gpus they run on will disappear.

It's all going to get sold to the next buyer who can also buy the research team to help build it further.

Whether openAI, Anthropic or whoever is still around in 10 years doesn't matter. AI is here and it's getting seriously good

62200178
The real question is whether she was in on it or not and was gonna take a cut of the emotional damage hush money…it’s a twist on the old wild things plot kinda

62200395
>>62197194
>I Love this Timeline
I Love this Timeline
>I Love this Timeline
I Love this timeline

62200424
>Two more witnesses showed up in the JPMorgan sex abuse case in the refiled legal suit by a former JPMorgan Chase banker, Chirayu Rana, against Lorna Hajdini on the Manhattan Supreme Court docket on Monday, May 4.
>The latest filing includes the testimony of a family friend who claims to have witnessed the intoxicated behaviour of Hajdini. This incident reportedly took place in a New York City apartment where Rana was invited for a threesome.
>Rana’s friend stated in the document that he was woken up by Hajdini in the middle of the night. The witness also mentioned that a “completely naked” Hajdini woke him while he was sleeping on the couch.
>She lit a cigarette and asked Rana to “join them” in the bedroom. When Rana refused, Hajdini allegedly told him, “You know I own [redacted], so you better come join.”
>The man further claimed that after she returned to the bedroom, he could hear Rana pleading, “No, no, no, you have to leave. I’m not going to do this. Please stop.”
>Thereafter, Hajdini left the apartment, and a stunned Rana emerged. The witness then claimed that Rana shared how Hajdini had involved him in sexual behaviour on multiple occasions.
>Rana also shared how Hajdini allegedly blackmailed him and put him in trouble if he refused to comply.
>Another witness, possibly the owner of the apartment where the family friend was staying, claimed he saw Hajdini kissing Rana’s neck on the street. This incident was reportedly from the summer of 2024.
>The witness also claimed that in September 2024, she overheard them entering her apartment building when Hajdini said something like, “I own you, Brownie.”

62200099
>>62200024
The suppression in this thread is crazy, I think we are onto something. Ain't a bot nigga bitch de la pizza française.

62200105
>state proofs
these require:
validator node software itself to be modified to communicate with the validators of other blockchains, which is both unscalable and a governance nightmare.

or a smart contract implementation with third party intermediaries, which brings us back to square one.

62201361
>>62200105

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 88 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-05-07 06:42 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188279
>>62186790
if the governments of the world totally shut off immigration to suit this reality, i'd not be concerned, the problem is they want to import an endless legion of turd world subhumans into the west at the same time as there's no real jobs left for the citizens. Whilst promoting their globohomo bloodsports in Ukraine and the ME.

>come white man, enlist! we only let you get the gig when we need goyim to die for lsraeI! heres you're chance!

62188591
>>62184289
>skill issue.
reddit post.

62188606
>>62184286

62188643
>>62184286

62188765

So my boss must be smarter than me...that's why im retiring in 3 years and that fucker will be slaving until 80

62188796

62188815
>>62188279

62188832
>>62188796

62188926

62188963

hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702

>>62187055

62190516
>>62185827
>>62188258

62190529
capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way

62192053
>>62188815
kys shill-rat.
>>62188832
agreed, thats why i hate kikes, they invented communism in their kike-think-tanks, weaponised it and unleashed it on Russia+china, and the world. Kikes are either directly, or indirectly, behind 95% of all human suffering for the last ~200 years.

62194013
>>62184286
Everyone is talking about that - on plebbit and normie sites. This board is for retards with $3000 saved to gamble it on shitcoins dreaming of becoming a millionaire.

62194027
>>62185045
>network engineer
>CompTIA
Doubt. Even a CCNA won't get you a job.
CompTIA is basically worthless.

62194055
>>62184286
>Ive been working for 10 years

Woah, woah, woah Hold up there gramps.

I haven't seen attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion, but I am getting video interviews at least. The problem is usually the company will cuck out at the last minute and cancel the req before I get a chance to start. I've been between jobs now for 8 months, almost 10% of your career, and it happens quite a bit. I suggest you pick up a book. Maybe pass the 3 CFA exams. Until everyone learns to stop being faggots about AI, we're in for a bumpy ride because galaxy brains like yourself think you can automate and forget it. Some businesses will have to get fucked up first THEN they can start hiring humans again

62194083
>>62188643
this is serious

>>62188963
you'll be poorer than you ever imagined soon

>>62190529
this. I would like to subscribe thank you

62194913
>>62194027
even tho the faggot you replied to was replying to me, I believe his story. I joined a contractor IT job and they had many clients whom I got to interact with. Every single one of them liked me and boasted about the work i did. I reached out to the one i liked most when I was ready to rejoin employment after a year break and they swooped me up with no interview or credential checking. I do not have any certs, dropped out of college since it was a meme and a scam and learned everything through projects I was doing based from my hobbies (gaming).
>certs, degrees, etc are useless in clown world
>proof of work always works, like BTC if your proof of work is amazing YOU WILL BE SOUGHT OUT
>after a year of interacting with AIs, i asked them to give me an IQ estimate based upon our interactions and all of them told me 135+
While you are worrying about wasting time "meeting" their requirements for work, employers are wasting time meeting my requirements to work for them. You and I are not the same

62197152
>>62184413
Try saying that in public to 1/4 of the population and see what happens.

62200034
>>62184286
there's a looooot of boomers still in the workforce, and the news now says they have more wealth than all other generations combined.

62200057
>>62185888
I decided I wanted to go to law school and then was brutally denied with a 167 while negresses who complied they only had a 151 were accepted. Not to good schools, mind you, but seeing some 30yo fat black chick making 20k a month in DC and complaining she can't make ends meet and finding out she only got a 155 on the LSAT but went to Harvard Law is just a massive FUCK you.

62200063
>>62186171
ironic since Shrub is the reason things got so bad.

>>62186790
Capitalism and eugenics are inherently at odds, social darwinism means I'm smart enough to bash you in with a rock while you cry as I execute your kids gang style. Capitalism is your daddy got you a nice job and you think you're a tough guy. These are at opposite ends of the value spectrum.

62200068
>>62194913
networking doesn't matter when you get your head bashed in with a rock.

62200076
>>62200034
there's a 75yo at the local mail room who glares at me when I come in, but is like sugar for every 20-30yo woman. He's a lazy fuck and a creep. These guys are one bad day from keeling over and they're still competing with us for women, our fathers/grandfathers, like it's 1975. I hate them so bad.

62200088
>>62200057
Fuck, really? What was your GPA?

62200155
>>62197152
Most people have a job NEETcel.

62200169
>>62200155
You do know that they've been consistently revising those numbers way down right? I have a job currently myself, but I know Jerome Powell himself was admitting that the job market is probably a lot worse than what it has looked like on the surface in the last couple years.

62200185
>>62184286
>Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course
I've been saying this before but this is the biggest class action law suit in American history waiting to happen. the sheer premeditated and coldhearted and quite honestly treasonous fraud perpetrated on the American labor force is monstrous in size and weight. the amount of time, energy, labor, and national resources wasted by corporate America is of such magnitude it's almost incomprehensible yet it still pales in comparison to the psychological and emotional damages and torture caused by callous greedy corporate monsters to the average American. it's not exaggeration to call the actions of corporations writing out fake job positions never to be filled as plainly one thing, and one thing only: job terrorism. the impact is not imagined, it is too real: innocent, hardworking Americans armed with nothing but a dream of a better life and the pursuit of happiness have committed suicide because of the irresponsible behavior of corporations. corporate America is guilty, guilty of treason, guilty of murder, guilty of the most heinous crimes in American history: any American citizen who has ever been invited to a ghost job interview is according to my estimations owed anywhere between $1-5 million dollars in damages paid. I'm not a lawyer nor is this financial advice nor should anything I write or say ever be taken seriously especially not in this ironic anime board but all I have to say is God Bless America and damn those evil ghost jobs to hell!

62200599
>>62185125
>>62185702
>>62186168
>>62190507
Trump did that

>>62197690

62197713

>>62197777

62198312

62198389

62198507

62198798

62198802

62198841

62198863
>>62197913

62198920
>>62197713

Why would anybody willingly move to the arctic version of Somalia?

62198981
>>62198013
It seriously is, I can hardly drive on the roads anymore and all I see are license plates from blue shitholes like NY and CaliforniaZ Alcoa highway is so congested compared to 6 years ago, it’s dismal. Every restaurant packed, every road a line of cars here in Knoxville.

62198989
>>62198013
>>62198981
Nashville already sucked in 2020 Knoxville is falling as we speak.

62199004
>>62197690
why do we consider chinks humans

62199028
>>62197690
Why do roasties hate the country so much and just want to live in the big cities?

62200217
>>62197811
America sucks i wish every day i was born in yurop

62200273
>>62197690

62200284
>>62198863
What braindrain? Americans are moving to europe.

62200286
>>62199028
Because they wish for the sperm of high quality men and they reside in cities.

62200301
>>62200286
Black men and Jews?

62200359
>>62197713
No, the taxes are fucked. Especially here in MPLS. If I wanted something midwestern, I would consider Kansas City. Maybe.
>>62198920
The Somalians generally stick to Minneapolis and Eden Prairie... maybe a couple other suburbs.

62200377
>>62197690
indiana probably

62200398
>>62200301
Also the high status chad white men.

62200560
>>62197690
Wow she is just like my ex

62200576
>>62200560
I'm jealous

62200994
>>62197690
She is standing in a clean large kitchen filled with electrical appliances, eating a wide variety of foods. Clean clothes, nice haircut, good teeth clear skin, and she is COMPLAINING about being poor.
99% of the rest of the world DREAMS of being this poor.

62200998
>>62197690
why do everyone in uscuck behave like nigs? meanwhile asians in japan are cute and not this fucking mentally ill

62201089
>>62200576
It wears you down anon

item.title: link can't even get to 10. it can't even fucking get to 10. you people SUCK lmao. just know that we're never gonna let y… — 13 replies — 7.8/hr

OP

link can't even get to 10. it can't even fucking get to 10. you people SUCK lmao. just know that we're never gonna let you live it down. you had your one big chance and you blew, it stinkers. now everything is so fucking expensive and you're still struggling to achieve prices that were bad 5 years ago. don't you get it stinkbags. YOU LOST THE GAME

Early replies (first 3)

62195921
>>62195555
Check the chart, you lost tranny

62195940
>>62195555
Checked and hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62196514
>>62195644

62196975
>>62195921
just did. sub 10
you lost, stinker

62200283
bump

62200651
Of all the avatars to assume to go along with the spam, why did the tranny pick a little girl?

62201619
>>62200651
shut up, stinkbag

62201632
$10 is a reasonable price for what chainlink does. People don't like to speculate on chainlink's price because it's not very volatile and most people don't even know what it does.

62201653
i sold at about $35 years ago since it was a 5x for me and i still can't believe people held after it came down from that massive pump

62201703
>>62201653
I took out my initial plus $10k at ~$37 and put the rest of my stack in the pool for rewards
If this shit ever moves again it's literally free money

62201709
>>62201653
>>62201703
this thread isn't a group therapy session, stinkers
just admit you lost the game or get out

62201783
>>62201709
poor fella fomo'd the top and got burned
many such cases

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 25 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-07 12:47 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62195653
>>62195300
Ok. Just do whatever you wanna do when it comes to your altcoins preferences. That matters.

62195854
>>62195275
BTC and altcoins all good

62195895
>>62195293
Higher anon

62195957
>>62195300

62195988
>we going to zero

62196046
>>62195653
How about you? What do you prefer

62196084
your pedo coin will die

62196112
>>62195373
Whut

62197011
you know calling something pedo is bullish, right

62197558
>>62195854
And which altcoins?

62199876
>>62195895
That's what I'm telling as well

62200331
>>62197011
Pedo pedo

62200336
>>62195275
Just buy Link and avax

62200341
>>62195988
LINK buy. I will keep telling everyone to buy this

62200348
>>62195275
>Btc
Eth
link
Sui
Sol
Bonk
Neo

Rate my bags

62200350
I will go all in Solana because it the most popular plataform for tokens. If you want to rugpull then you will use Solana System.

Bitcoin can`t 5x in 2 years. There is not enough money in the market for that.

62201146
>>62200350
All in or bust

62201148
>>62200341
Then nerve

62201149
>>62195957
Ironically, eth might be useful if only because jews will push it back to 6k in the next cycle. From a 1500 bottom to 6k, that's 4x in guaranteed returns. if I had 300k in my pocket I'd probably pick eth.

62201348
>>62201149
Let my whale friend pump ETH

62201349
>>62200348
Neo is an old bag kek

62201352
>>62201149
